name: HashiCorp Vault
description: Secrets management platform for storing, dynamically generating and brokering access to credentials, with policy-based access control and detailed audit devices. Evidence covers audit device configuration, audit logs, policies, auth methods and lease/TTL settings.

L1.title: Vault Manual Configuration and Audit Log Collection

steps.action: Authenticate to Vault with a dedicated auditor token (vault login), then run 'vault audit list -detailed' to capture the enabled audit devices and their configuration (type, file path, log format)
steps.permissions_required: Vault token with a policy granting read and sudo on sys/audit
steps.security_note: Never use the root token for evidence collection — use a token bound to a least-privilege auditor policy
steps.audit_note: Evidence that at least one audit device is enabled demonstrates all authenticated requests and responses are being logged

steps.action: Run 'vault auth list -detailed' to export the enabled auth methods with their accessors, default TTLs, max TTLs and token types
steps.permissions_required: Vault token with read and sudo on sys/auth
steps.audit_note: Auth method inventory evidences which authentication mechanisms can mint Vault tokens and their lifetime limits

steps.action: Run 'vault policy list' followed by 'vault policy read <name>' for each key policy, saving the HCL output to date-stamped files
steps.permissions_required: Vault token with read on sys/policies/acl and list on sys/policies/acl
steps.audit_note: Policy documents are the authoritative statement of who may access which secret paths and with what capabilities

steps.action: Run 'vault secrets list -detailed' to export the mounted secrets engines including their default and max lease TTLs per mount
steps.permissions_required: Vault token with read and sudo on sys/mounts
steps.audit_note: Per-mount TTL settings evidence credential lifetime controls for dynamic secrets

steps.action: Extract a sample of audit log entries covering the collection period from the file audit device path (e.g. the last 7 days from /var/log/vault/vault_audit.log)
steps.permissions_required: Operating system read access to the audit log path on the Vault host
steps.security_note: Sensitive values in audit logs are HMAC-hashed by Vault, but logs still reveal paths, identities and metadata — handle as confidential
steps.audit_note: Audit entries are timestamped by Vault and hashed values allow verification without exposing secrets

steps.action: Upload the command outputs and audit log sample to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Review outputs for any accidentally captured plaintext secrets or tokens before uploading

L2.title: Vault Scheduled Configuration Snapshots and Rotated Audit Logs

steps.action: Vault has no built-in scheduled report or export feature — the closest reliable alternative is a scheduled job (cron/systemd timer) running read-only Vault CLI commands, combined with rotated audit log files
steps.audit_note: Documenting this design decision evidences a deliberate, repeatable collection process

steps.action: Create a dedicated auditor policy granting read (and sudo where required) on sys/audit, sys/auth, sys/mounts and sys/policies/acl, and issue a renewable token bound to it for the scheduled job
steps.permissions_required: Vault administrator able to write policies and create tokens
steps.security_note: Bind the token to the auditor policy only, set a bounded TTL, and store it in the host's credential store — never in the crontab or scripts

steps.action: Schedule the job to run 'vault audit list -detailed -format=json', 'vault auth list -format=json', 'vault secrets list -detailed -format=json' and 'vault policy list -format=json', writing date-stamped JSON snapshots to the evidence share
steps.permissions_required: Token bound to the auditor policy
steps.audit_note: Machine-generated JSON snapshots on a fixed cadence give consistent, comparable configuration evidence

steps.action: Configure log rotation (e.g. logrotate) for the file audit device path so each period produces a closed, date-stamped audit log file, using a copy-then-truncate or SIGHUP-based rotation that Vault supports
steps.permissions_required: Operating system administrator on the Vault host
steps.audit_note: Closed, rotated log files are effectively immutable period records — verify no events are lost at rotation boundaries

steps.action: Deliver the snapshots and rotated audit logs to the evidence repository automatically, and record each cycle's completion and period coverage in the evidence tracker
steps.security_note: Restrict the evidence share to authorised compliance personnel — audit logs contain identity and access metadata

L3.title: Vault API-Driven Automated Collection via AppRole

steps.action: Enable the AppRole auth method ('vault auth enable approle') and create a role for the collection service with token_policies set to the auditor policy and a short token_ttl (e.g. 20m)
steps.permissions_required: Vault administrator with write on sys/auth and auth/approle
steps.security_note: AppRole gives the collector machine identity without embedding a long-lived human token

steps.action: Write the auditor policy in HCL granting only the paths needed, e.g. path "sys/audit" { capabilities = ["read", "sudo"] }, plus read on sys/auth and sys/mounts and read/list on sys/policies/acl
steps.permissions_required: Vault administrator with write on sys/policies/acl
steps.audit_note: A least-privilege collection policy is itself evidence of access control discipline

steps.action: Configure the collection script to authenticate with POST /v1/auth/approle/login (role_id + secret_id), then call GET /v1/sys/audit, GET /v1/sys/auth, GET /v1/sys/mounts and LIST /v1/sys/policies/acl with the X-Vault-Token header, storing responses as date-stamped JSON
steps.permissions_required: AppRole role_id and secret_id bound to the auditor policy
steps.audit_note: The /v1/sys/mounts response includes default_lease_ttl and max_lease_ttl per mount — direct evidence of credential lifetime controls

steps.action: Store the role_id and secret_id in a secrets store on the collector, use response wrapping when delivering the secret_id, and set secret_id_ttl and secret_id_num_uses to bound its exposure
steps.security_note: Never persist the secret_id in plaintext config; rotate it on schedule and rely on the short-lived login token for each run

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, including 403 responses that indicate policy drift or an expired/revoked secret_id
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should be logged and trigger re-collection within SLA

steps.action: Validate each run by diffing the collected audit devices, auth methods, mounts and policy list against the approved baseline, flagging any unexpected additions, removals or TTL changes
steps.audit_note: Baseline drift detection turns configuration snapshots into continuous change evidence

L4.title: Vault Continuous Audit Streaming Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Enable a second audit device streaming to your SIEM collector — a socket audit device ('vault audit enable socket address=<collector>:<port> socket_type=tcp') or syslog device — alongside the existing file device
steps.permissions_required: Vault token with sudo on sys/audit
steps.security_note: Keep at least two audit devices enabled: Vault blocks all requests if every audit device fails to write, so a resilient pair protects both availability and evidence continuity
steps.audit_note: Streaming plus file gives near-real-time evidence with a durable local fallback

steps.action: Alternatively (or additionally) ship the file audit device output with a log forwarder (e.g. Fluent Bit, Splunk forwarder) to the SIEM, following Vault's audit logging best practices for rotation and delivery guarantees
steps.permissions_required: Operating system administrator on the Vault host plus SIEM administrator
steps.audit_note: Forwarding the file device avoids the blocking risk of a slow network audit device while still centralising evidence

steps.action: Configure completeness metrics in the SIEM: audit events per day, ingestion latency, and per-node coverage across the Vault cluster, cross-checked against Vault telemetry and the health endpoint (GET /v1/sys/health)
steps.permissions_required: SIEM administrator; unauthenticated or minimally privileged access for /v1/sys/health
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Set up automated quality checks — validate audit entry schema, confirm sensitive fields remain HMAC-hashed, detect gaps in the event time series and duplicate request IDs
steps.audit_note: Gap and duplicate detection on request-level audit data underpins the integrity claim for the whole pipeline

steps.action: Create a dashboard showing pipeline health (events/day per audit device, latency, blocked-device alerts, failed forwarder deliveries) alongside daily configuration snapshot status from the L3 collector
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of process health

steps.action: Implement an automated quarterly review that reconciles SIEM event counts against rotated file audit logs, generates a collection health report and flags any SLA breaches or unexplained gaps
steps.audit_note: Independent reconciliation between the streaming and file devices validates end-to-end completeness
